domain: Egyptian shaduf (~2200 BC; long seesaw lever with bucket on long end + counterweight on short end; operator pulls bucket down to fill; counterweight automatically lifts; bistable between rest positions; ~60% efficiency)
specific_mechanism: Lever-balanced active-passive complementarity — operator effort fills the bucket, counterweight stores complementary potential energy; release returns system to opposite rest position; bistable.
llm_application: SHADUF-OPTIMIZER: a per-parameter optimizer modification that accumulates a 'counterweight' c_t from opposite-sign gradients. For each parameter θ_i, maintain c_t = β c_{t−1} + (1−β) × |g_t| × 1[sign(g_t) ≠ sign(c_{t−1})] (counterweight grows when current gradient opposes accumulated direction). Update u_t = lr × (g_t − γ × c_t × sign(g_{t−1})). The counterweight passively dampens oscillation in narrow loss valleys by storing opposite-direction force that automatically reverses when load releases. Different from Momentum (same-direction accumulation), Adam (squared-grad scale), GradNorm (cross-task balance), SHADUF-OPTIMIZER explicitly maintains an OPPOSITE-DIRECTION reserve that pushes the optimizer back when over-shooting.
novelty_claim: Momentum accumulates same-direction gradient history. Adam normalizes by squared-grad. GradNorm balances task losses by normalizing across tasks. AdamW decouples weight decay. GaLore projects gradients into low-rank. SHADUF-OPTIMIZER differs by (a) OPPOSITE-SIGN COUNTERWEIGHT ACCUMULATOR per parameter, (b) bistable update rule with passive anti-oscillation force, (c) lever-balance interpretation. R092 was anti-correlated NOISE injection; SHADUF is PASSIVE counterweight from gradient HISTORY not noise.
